Package org.rodinp.internal.core

Class RodinDB

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.core.runtime.PlatformObject
org.rodinp.core.basis.RodinElement
org.rodinp.internal.core.Openable
org.rodinp.internal.core.RodinDB
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IOpenable, IParent, IRodinDB, IRodinElement
public class RodinDB extends Openable implements IRodinDB
Implementation of IRodinDB. The Rodin database maintains a cache of active IRodinProjects in a workspace. A Rodin database is specific to a workspace. To retrieve a workspace's database, use the #getRodinDB(IWorkspace) method.
See Also:
  • IRodinDB

  • Constructor Details

    • RodinDB

      protected RodinDB() throws Error
      Constructs a new Rodin database on the given workspace. Note that only one instance of RodinDB handle should ever be created. One should only indirect through RodinDBManager#getRodinDB() to get access to it.
      Throws:
      Error - if called more than once

  • Method Details

    • buildStructure

      protected boolean buildStructure(OpenableElementInfo info, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor pm, RodinDBManager.OpenableMap newElements, org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource underlyingResource)
      Description copied from class: Openable
      Builds this element's structure and properties in the given info object, based on this element's current contents (reuse buffer contents if this element has an open buffer, or resource contents if this element does not have an open buffer). Children are placed in the given newElements table (note, this element has already been placed in the newElements table). Returns true if successful, or false if an error is encountered while determining the structure of this element.
      Specified by:
      buildStructure in class Openable

    • contains

      public boolean contains(org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource resource)
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Returns whether this Rodin database contains an IRodinElement whose resource is the given resource or a non-Rodin resource which is the given resource.

      Note: no existency check is performed on the argument resource. If it is not accessible (see IResource.isAccessible()) yet but would be located in Rodin database range, then it will return true.

      If the resource is accessible, it can be reached by navigating the Rodin database down using the getChildren() and/or getNonRodinResources() methods.

      Specified by:
      contains in interface IRodinDB
      Parameters:
      resource - the resource to check
      Returns:
      true if the resource is accessible through the Rodin database

    • copy

      public void copy(IRodinElement[] elements, IRodinElement[] containers, IRodinElement[] siblings, String[] renamings, boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Copies the given elements to the specified container(s). If one container is specified, all elements are copied to that container. If more than one container is specified, the number of elements and containers must match, and each element is copied to its associated container.

      Optionally, each copy can be positioned before a sibling element. If null is specified for a given sibling, the copy is inserted as the last child of its associated container.

      Optionally, each copy can be renamed. If null is specified for the new name, the copy is not renamed.

      Optionally, any existing child in the destination container with the same name can be replaced by specifying true for force. Otherwise an exception is thrown in the event that a name collision occurs.

      Specified by:
      copy in interface IRodinDB
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements to copy
      containers - the container, or list of containers
      siblings - the list of sibling elements, any of which may be null; or null
      renamings - the list of new names, any of which may be null; or null
      force - true if any existing child in a target container with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an element could not be copied. Reasons include:
      • There is no element to process (NO_ELEMENTS_TO_PROCESS). The given elements is null or empty
      • A specified element, container, or sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • A container is of an incompatible type (INVALID_DESTINATION)
      • A sibling is not a child of it associated container (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • A new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in its associated container already exists with the same name and replace has been specified as false (NAME_COLLISION)
      • The number of renamings does not match the number of elements (INVALID_RENAMING)
      • A container or element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • A destination element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • createElementInfo

      protected RodinDBInfo createElementInfo()
      Returns a new element info for this element.
      Specified by:
      createElementInfo in class Openable

    • delete

      public void delete(IRodinElement[] elements, boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Deletes the given elements, forcing the operation if necessary and specified.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface IRodinDB
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements to delete
      force - a flag controlling whether underlying resources that are not in sync with the local file system will be tolerated
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an element could not be deleted. Reasons include:
      • There is no element to process ( NO_ELEMENTS_TO_PROCESS). The given elements is null or empty
      • A specified element does not exist ( ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • An element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • An element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Description copied from class: RodinElement
      Returns true if this handle represents the same Rodin element as the given handle. By default, two handles represent the same element if they are identical or if they represent the same type of element, have equal names and parents.

      If a subclass has other requirements for equality, this method must be overridden.

      Overrides:
      equals in class RodinElement
      See Also:
      • Object.equals(java.lang.Object)

      public IRodinProject findOldRodinProject(org.eclipse.core.resources.IProject project)
      Finds the given project in the list of the Rodin database's children. Returns null if not found.

      Returns the list of Rodin projects before resource delta processing has started.

    • getRodinProject

      public IRodinProject getRodinProject(String projectName)
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Returns the Rodin project with the given name. This is a handle-only method. The project may or may not exist.
      Specified by:
      getRodinProject in interface IRodinDB
      Parameters:
      projectName - the name of the Rodin project
      Returns:
      the Rodin project with the given name

    • getRodinProject

      public RodinProject getRodinProject(org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource resource)
      Returns the active Rodin project associated with the specified resource, or null if no Rodin project yet exists for the resource.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given resource is not one of an IProject, IFolder, or IFile.

    • getRodinProjects

      public IRodinProject[] getRodinProjects() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Returns the Rodin projects in this Rodin database, or an empty array if there are none.
      Specified by:
      getRodinProjects in interface IRodinDB
      Returns:
      the Rodin projects in this Rodin database, or an empty array if there are none
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this request fails.

    • getNonRodinResources

      public org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource[] getNonRodinResources() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Returns an array of non-Rodin resources (that is, non-Rodin projects) in the workspace.

      Non-Rodin projects include all projects that are closed (even if they have the Rodin nature).

      Specified by:
      getNonRodinResources in interface IRodinDB
      Returns:
      an array of non-Rodin projects (IProjects) contained in the workspace.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • getPath

      public org.eclipse.core.runtime.IPath getPath()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element. The path returned is the full, absolute path to the underlying resource, relative to the workspace.

      This is a handle-only method.

      This is a convenience method fully equivalent to getOpenable().getFullPath().

      Specified by:
      getPath in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element

    • getResource

      public org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource getResource()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the innermost resource enclosing this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getResource in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the innermost resource enclosing this element

      public org.eclipse.core.resources.IWorkspaceRoot getUnderlyingResource()

      Returns the smallest underlying resource that contains this element, or null if this element is not contained in a resource.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getUnderlyingResource in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the underlying resource, or null if none

    • getElementName

      public String getElementName()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the name of this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      getElementName in interface IRodinElement
      Specified by:
      getElementName in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      the element name
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • move

      public void move(IRodinElement[] elements, IRodinElement[] containers, IRodinElement[] siblings, String[] renamings, boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Moves the given elements to the specified container(s). If one container is specified, all elements are moved to that container. If more than one container is specified, the number of elements and containers must match, and each element is moved to its associated container.

      Optionally, each element can be positioned before a sibling element. If null is specified for sibling, the element is inserted as the last child of its associated container.

      Optionally, each element can be renamed. If null is specified for the new name, the element is not renamed.

      Optionally, any existing child in the destination container with the same name can be replaced by specifying true for force. Otherwise an exception is thrown in the event that a name collision occurs.

      Specified by:
      move in interface IRodinDB
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements to move
      containers - the container, or list of containers
      siblings - the list of siblings element any of which may be null; or null
      renamings - the list of new names any of which may be null; or null
      force - true if any existing child in a target container with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an element could not be moved. Reasons include:
      • There is no element to process (NO_ELEMENTS_TO_PROCESS). The given elements is null or empty
      • A specified element, container, or sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • A container is of an incompatible type (INVALID_DESTINATION)
      • A sibling is not a child of it associated container (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • A new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in its associated container already exists with the same name and replace has been specified as false (NAME_COLLISION)
      • The number of renamings does not match the number of elements (INVALID_RENAMING)
      • A container or element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • A source or destination element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • rename

      public void rename(IRodinElement[] elements, String[] names, boolean replace, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinDB
      Renames the given elements as specified.
      Specified by:
      rename in interface IRodinDB
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements to rename
      names - the list of new names. Must not be null. Must have the same length as elements.
      replace - true if an existing element with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an element could not be renamed. Reasons include:
      • There is no element to process (NO_ELEMENTS_TO_PROCESS). The given elements is null or empty
      • A specified element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • A new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child already exists with the same name and replace has been specified as false (NAME_COLLISION)
      • The number of renamings does not match the number of elements (INVALID_RENAMING)
      • An element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • An element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)
